content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Pashmak and Parmida's problem". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/459/problem/D
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then for each test case: The first line contains two integers n and k, the number of elements and the target sum. The second line contains n integers, the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum number of operations required to make all elements of the array equal to the target sum.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Sort the array and then use a two-pointer technique to find the minimum number of operations required.

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    n, k = map(int, input().split())
    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    arr.sort()
    ans = sum(abs(x - k) for x in arr)
    print(ans)
